# init(fromGridStartingAt:width:height:diagonalsAllowed:)

**Initializer**

Initializes a graph that describes an integer grid with the specified dimensions.

## Declaration

```swift
init(fromGridStartingAt position: vector_int2, width: Int32, height: Int32, diagonalsAllowed: Bool)
```

## Parameters

- **position**: The lowest x- and y-coordinates to appear in the grid.
- **width**: The number of possible x-coordinates in the grid.
- **height**: The number of possible y-coordinates in the grid.
- **diagonalsAllowed**: [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] to connect nodes in the grid to their diagonal neighbors; [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false] to connect nodes only to their horizontal and vertical neighbors.

## Return Value

A new grid graph.

## Discussion

All connections created through this method are bidirectional.

## Creating a Graph

- **init(fromGridStartingAt:width:height:diagonalsAllowed:nodeClass:)**: Initializes a graph that describes an integer grid with the specified dimensions, using the specified node class.
